summary: Thick skin is found on palms and soles; thin skin covers the rest of the body.
Thick skin covers the scalp and back; thin skin covers the palms.
Thick skin has hair follicles while thin skin lacks them.
A dermatome is the area of skin supplied by a single spinal nerve.
A dermatome is the group of muscles supplied by a single spinal nerve.
A dermatome is the area of bone supplied by a single nutrient artery.
Surgical incisions along Langer's lines heal with minimal scarring due to collagen fibre orientation.
Langer's lines run perpendicular to collagen fibres in the dermis.
Incisions across Langer's lines heal with less scarring than those along them.
The superficial fascia (subcutaneous tissue) contains fat, cutaneous nerves, and superficial vessels.
The deep fascia contains subcutaneous fat and cutaneous nerves.
The superficial fascia is a tough membrane that wraps muscles.
The dermis has two layers: the superficial papillary layer and the deeper reticular layer.
The dermis has three layers: papillary, reticular, and subcutaneous.
The epidermis has two layers: papillary and reticular.
The epidermis is keratinised stratified squamous epithelium containing melanocytes.
The epidermis is simple columnar epithelium with goblet cells.
The dermis is stratified squamous epithelium containing melanocytes.
The skin weighs approximately 3.5 kg and covers about 2 square metres in an adult.
The skin weighs approximately 10 kg and covers about 5 square metres.
The skin weighs approximately 1 kg and covers about 1 square metre.
